message:
fix(messaging): validate partition numbers and reject duplicate entries in Kafka consumer

Three categories of input validation added to KafkaConsumer:

1. Negative partition rejection: new validate_partition_number() helper
   rejects negative i32 partition values in rebalance(), commit_offsets(),
   and seek(). Previously negative partitions could silently enter the
   assignment/commit maps.

2. Duplicate detection: rebalance() and commit_offsets() now use a
   BTreeMap to normalize entries and reject duplicate topic/partition
   pairs in the same batch. Previously the last entry silently won.

3. commit_offsets() refactored to collect into normalized map first,
   then apply atomically, matching rebalance()'s pattern.

New tests: consumer_rebalance_rejects_duplicate_partition_entries,
consumer_rebalance_rejects_negative_partition_numbers (+ commit/seek).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
